risk: Simulation tire model does not match the built tire operating window.
impact: High; tire parameters dominate envelope, lateral limit, braking, and yaw authority.
early_warning: Track tire temperatures, pressure rise, or slip behavior disagree with predicted window.
mitigation: Collect ISO 4138 slow-ramp data, update tire model, rerun EnvelopeSim and StandardSim.

risk: Aero map or ride-height state differs from the assumed baseline.
impact: High; aero load moves GGV volume, cornering, lateral limit, and balance.
early_warning: Balance changes strongly with speed/rake or ride heights differ from simulated values.
mitigation: Measure ride heights dynamically where possible and run aero/load/rake sweeps.

risk: Near-zero understeer gradient causes percent sensitivity values to look exaggerated.
impact: Medium; ranking may be visually dramatic even when absolute response is modest.
early_warning: Tornado percent spans are very large for small absolute balance changes.
mitigation: Use native-unit span, sign, driver feel, and refined surfaces as the engineering read.

risk: Rear-drive acceleration and four-wheel braking are confused in interpretation.
impact: Medium; acceleration and braking limits have different available tire sets and load-transfer behavior.
early_warning: Accel and brake comparisons are described without drive/brake architecture context.
mitigation: Notebook explicitly states rear-only drive and both-axle braking assumptions.

risk: Setup recommendation is overfit to a single objective.
impact: Medium; maximizing one metric can hurt stability, effort, tire temperature, or endurance consistency.
early_warning: A single best variant is presented without constraints or tradeoff surfaces.
mitigation: Use refined surfaces as regions, then overlay build limits, thermal behavior, and driver feedback.
